rulings.Case Title: MICKEY Y WONG VS. DORIS WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Doris Wongs Notice Of Motion Motion For An Order Barring Plaintiff From Offering Any Documents At Trial Other Than Documents Previously Produced Because Of His Disobedience Of Court Order; Request For Sanctions.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 13, 2015, line 1. Defendant Doris Wong's Motion For An Order Barring Plaintiff From Offering Any Documents At Trial Other Than Documents Previously Produced Because Of His Disobedience Of Court Order; Request For Sanctions DENIED. The moving party failed to show that there was both a failure to comply and that failure was willful. (R.S. Creative Inc. v. Creative Cotton, Ltd., 75 Cal. App. 4th 486, 496 (1999).) = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROCHELLE VAUGHN et al VS. WALTER WORSHAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Recover Costs Of Service Where Mail Service Refused
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 13, 2015, line 2. PLAINTIFFS ROCHELLE VAUGN, MIGUEL WASHINGTON AND REYNA WASHINGTONS' Motion To Recover Costs Of Service Where Mail Service Refused GRANTED. Plaintiffs shall recover costs in the amount of $989.40. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS PANOUTSOPOULOS et al VS. THE KARSANT FAMILY LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants And Cross-Complainants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 13, 2015, line 3. DEFENDANT THE KARSANT FAMILY LIMITED PARTNERSHIP AND Defendants And Cross-Complainant PETER KARSANTS' Motion For Protective Order DENIED. Moving party failed to show that any information regarding events pre-dating April 15, 2013 is irrelevant, burdensome, or that discovery is barred by collateral estoppel. Sanctions are denied. Reply is stricken as it was not authorized by April 29, 2015 order. Opposing party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B in future. = (501/REQ)
